message:
fix: use split_prefix_remainder for hyphenated issue ID prefixes

The previous prefix-detection logic used `split_once('-')` or
`find('-')` to extract the prefix from an issue ID, which broke on
multi-segment prefixes like "document-intelligence-0sa" — those
would be truncated to just "document" instead of preserving the full
"document-intelligence" prefix.

Three call-sites are updated to use `split_prefix_remainder()` from
`crate::util::id`, which correctly splits at the *last* hyphen
separating the prefix from the hex remainder:

- `detect_prefix_from_jsonl` in sync.rs (import path)
- `first_prefix_from_jsonl` in config/mod.rs (single-issue inference)
- `common_prefix_from_jsonl` in config/mod.rs (multi-issue consensus)

The auto-import expected-prefix resolution in main.rs is also changed
from reading the raw `issue_prefix` DB key to calling
`resolve_auto_import_expected_prefix`, which loads the full merged
config layer stack (config.yaml > DB > CLI overrides). This ensures
the YAML-declared prefix wins over a stale DB value, which matters
when a project switches to a hyphenated prefix in config.yaml but
the DB still holds the old short prefix.

Tests added for all three JSONL helpers and for the merged-config
resolution path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
